intelligence_report.executive_summary:
Synthesis Report (Round 1, ref:b3799541):

## Executive Summary
Multi-agent analysis yields cautiously optimistic assessment. Research identifies 30-45% efficiency gains; critique flags methodological limitations and bias risks; strategy resolves tensions through phased deployment.

## Key Findings
key_findings: Evidence supports efficacy in controlled settings
Bias and equity concerns require proactive auditing
Phased deployment with validation gates recommended
intelligence_report.risk_assessment: MODERATE — strong pilot evidence tempered by generalizability unknowns
strategic_recommendations: Phase 1: Controlled pilot with matched cohort design (3 months)
Phase 2: Human-in-the-loop deployment with continuous monitoring (5 months)
Phase 3: Scale based on validated outcomes (4 months)
Mandatory fairness auditing across demographic subgroups
intelligence_report.evidence_quality: Mixed — strong in controlled settings, weak for production generalizability
